chapters.title: The Storm-Battered House
chapters.caption: The brooding figure of Heathcliff stands beneath a bare tree at the remote, wind-blasted farmhouse of Wuthering Heights.
chapters.attribution: Fritz Eichenberg, wood engraving from the 1943 Random House edition of Wuthering Heights. Public domain (published in the US without copyright notice between 1931–1977).

chapters.title: The Foundling
chapters.caption: A solitary figure moves through a moody autumn moorland under a heavy, overcast sky — the wild landscape that shaped Catherine and Heathcliff's childhood bond.
chapters.attribution: John Atkinson Grimshaw, Late October (1882). Oil on card. Public domain.

chapters.title: Thrushcross Grange
chapters.caption: A young woman pauses at ornate park gates in her fine clothes, poised between two worlds — the untamed moors behind her and the polished refinement of the Linton estate ahead.
chapters.attribution: John Atkinson Grimshaw, At The Park Gate (1878). Oil on canvas. Public domain.

chapters.title: The Return
chapters.caption: A moonlit lane gleams under a wet, cold sky — the desolate nocturnal atmosphere of Heathcliff's return and his agonised final reunion with the dying Catherine.
chapters.attribution: John Atkinson Grimshaw, A Wet Moon, Putney Road (1886). Oil on canvas. Public domain.

chapters.title: Death and Revenge
chapters.caption: Dark woodland paths wind beneath bare autumn trees, evoking the grim, inexorable march of Heathcliff's revenge over the two families.
chapters.attribution: John Atkinson Grimshaw, Woodland near Leeds (1869). Oil on canvas. Public domain.

chapters.title: The Ghost Appeased
chapters.caption: Young Hareton and his cousin Cathy sit together in a quiet moment of growing tenderness, their bond offering the novel's sole note of redemptive hope.
chapters.attribution: Edna Clarke Hall, Hareton and his Cousin. Watercolour. Public domain.
